nameMeanings.name: Moody
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: nickname from Middle English mody ‘proud, haughty, angry, fierce, bold, brave, or rash’ (Old English mōdig). Compare Muddiman . This English name has been established in Ireland since the late 13th century.

variants.name: Woody
meanings: from Middle English wode ‘wood’ + heie ‘enclosure’ (Old English wudu + (ge)hæg). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived by an enclosure in a wood, or habitational, for a person from a place so named, such as Woodhey Green in Faddiley (Cheshire). Also possibly from East Woodhay (Hampshire) and West Woodhay (Berkshire), apparently named with Old English wīd ‘wide, broad’ as the initial element, confused with early Old English widu ‘wood’, and so too with Old English wudu.

nickname from Middle English wodi or some other derivative of Middle English wode ‘frenzied, wild’ (Old English wōd), perhaps formed on the analogy of, for example, Middle English mody (see Moody ). Compare Wood 2.

variants.name: Muddiman
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): nickname from Middle English mody ‘proud, haughty, angry, fierce, bold, brave, rash’ (Old English mōdig) + man ‘man’. See Moody .

variants.name: Mooty
meanings: English: variant of   Moody  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain.
Possibly an Americanized form of German Mutig, a nickname meaning ‘cheerful’.

variants.name: Moodie
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Moody  .

variants.name: Moudy
meanings: English (London): variant of   Moody  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Mowdy
meanings: English or Irish: variant of   Moody  .

variants.name: Mudie
meanings: English and Scottish (Angus): variant of   Moody  .

nameMeanings.name: Edmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + mund ‘protector’. It was borne by several early royal and saintly figures, including a 9th-century king of East Anglia killed by invading Danes, allegedly for his adherence to Christianity. In the 16th and 17th centuries, there was a good deal of interchange between Edmund and Edward.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Edmund Moody was born in 1478. He married Mary Holden in 1498, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1562, in St Edmund's Church, Southwold, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 84.
